results.caption:
They didn't just build malware. They built a brand.

DragonForce emerged on BreachForums on December 6, 2023 — not as a tool, but as a cartel. A franchise. A logo. A revenue model.

363 confirmed victims. 30+ countries. 212.5% surge in attacks. And a business split that any startup would recognize: affiliates keep 80%, the cartel takes 20%.

This isn't hacking. This is organized crime with a marketing department.

The dragon isn't mythology. It's a business model.

#DragonForce #Ransomware #CyberSecurity #ThreatIntelligence #RaaS
results.hook: A mythic dragon forged from ransomware code creates instant visual identity for a threat actor most security teams still cannot define.

results.topic: The Binary Signature — Ransom Note Decoded
results.caption:
This is what your screen looks like after DragonForce gets in.

The note appears. The clock starts. The negotiation begins.

Behind that note: BYOVD attacks using truesight.sys and rentdrv2.sys to blind your EDR before you even know they're inside. ChaCha8 encryption deployed cross-platform — Windows, Linux, ESXi — simultaneously. No escape route.

Marks & Spencer. Co-op. Harrods. Three British retail giants. One summer. £300 million in estimated impact.

The ransom note isn't the attack. It's the receipt.

#DragonForce #RansomwareAttack #BYOVD #CyberDefense #IncidentResponse
results.hook: A forensic close-up of an actual ransomware note dissected like a crime scene autopsy makes the abstract threat viscerally real for every business leader scrolling LinkedIn.

results.caption:
Where does DragonForce come from?

Nobody can confirm it.

Some researchers point to Malaysia. Others find traces that suggest otherwise. The group itself leaves contradictions by design — the obfuscation is part of the operation.

What IS confirmed: They appeared on BreachForums on December 6, 2023. They announced their cartel model on March 19, 2025. They absorbed RansomHub's infrastructure on April 8, 2025. And they hit 363 organizations across 30+ countries.

The origin is unknown. The damage is not.

Attribution is the hardest problem in cybersecurity. DragonForce knows this.

#ThreatIntelligence #DragonForce #CyberAttribution #NationStateHacking #Ransomware
results.hook: The shadowy unmasking of an anonymous threat actor whose true origin remains unverified taps into the deepest fear in threat intelligence — the enemy you cannot locate.
